title: The Sacred Geometry, Architect of Perfect Forms
subtitle: Harmony & Immutable Law

lore.description: The Sacred Geometry teaches that perfect forms underlie all seeming chaos: the divine ratios that steer the spiral of galaxies, the symmetry that lattices frost, the measures that turn sound into song. Not merely a calculus but the hidden architecture of the world, it makes artists of order from those who serve it. Devotees do not count to chill the heart; they weigh and tune until beauty and structure converge, revealing that disorder is often only unrecognized design waiting to be drawn true.
lore.followers: This philosophy finds its purest expression in the game of Kon'reh — the living geometry played on an eight-pointed diamond board, where Apexes, Sanctums, and the Cross teach the dance of form and consequence. Yet the Geometry itself is not the game; the game is a mirror of the Geometry. The Ykrul favor Kon'reh as a tool for oath-geometry and battle-field prediction, reading the angles of feuds and the arcs of raids. The Aelinnel, by contrast, use it to navigate thresholds, courtesy, and the hidden patterns of fae bargains — they count steps not to win, but to avoid stepping where the Green Gate listens. To master Kon'reh is to glimpse the Sacred Geometry in motion; to serve the Geometry is to play the game with the world itself, whether by the steppe fire or the hawthorn arch.

names_across_regions.aeler: The Architect's Hand — True-Masons carve its ratios into keystones, believing that a properly proportioned arch can outlast any curse.
names_across_regions.ykrul: The Grammar of Honor — every Kon'reh move is a lesson in sacrifice and exit.
names_across_regions.thepyrgos: The Geometer's Measure — disciples who measure the city's staircases to keep the Hollow from noticing a missing step.

lore.quote: In the drift of atoms, the sweep of planets, and the curve of a lover's smile, the same ratios ring. Learn them, and you may conduct the world's quiet music.

domain_focus: Perfect Proportion: building and reading ideal ratios and harmonious relationships
Pattern Recognition: discerning the structures that govern complex phenomena
Harmonic Resonance: working with natural frequencies and number-made consonance
Form Architecture: imposing clear structure on tangled situations
Kon'reh Discipline: the game as meditation, ritual, and tactical mirror

thiasos.description: A Runekeeper sworn to the Sacred Geometry does not bind a familiar of flesh and blood. Their Thiasos is a living embodiment of perfect form: a small brass beetle whose wing-cases are etched with golden-ratio spirals, a mechanical hummingbird that hovers in mathematically precise arcs, a chameleon whose skin shifts in symmetrical patterns, a compass spider (a tiny arachnid that builds webs in perfect hexagons), or a sentient set of Kon'reh pieces that clack together to spell out advice.
thiasos.care: This creature requires no food, but it must be allowed to trace geometric figures (real or imagined) each day. If neglected for a week, it becomes Compromised (its patterns grow erratic, imposing –1 die on all Lore and Investigation rolls involving measurement or analysis until it is recalibrated).

codex.description: The Codex of the Sacred Geometry is never a normal book. It may be a set of brass stencils of Platonic solids, a roll of vellum covered in interlaced tessellations, a slate tablet on which proofs can be drawn and erased, the inside of a Kon'reh board, or a set of crystal lenses that project perfect geometric shapes when held to the light.
codex.upkeep: Because the Geometry's truths are relationships, the Codex requires upkeep: the Runekeeper must spend an hour each week redrawing or re-proving at least one theorem, or the Rites stored there become tangled (treat as Compromised until re-proved).

patrons_gift.name: Geometry's Blessing
patrons_gift.description: Once per scene as an action, a Runekeeper of the Sacred Geometry may touch a held item (weapon, shield, or tool) and inscribe a mental proportion upon it.
patrons_gift.effect: For the rest of the scene, the item grants +1 die to Craft or Investigation rolls involving structures, patterns, or measurements, and the first time each scene the bearer would be disoriented (by fog, maze, or supernatural confusion), they may ignore it — the Geometry holds true.
patrons_gift.cost: After the scene, the user marks +1 Obligation to the Sacred Geometry.

rites.name: Rite of the Harmonic Alignment
rites.materials: A tool calibrated to pristine ratios (e.g., golden ratio gauge, well-cut compass arc, a Kon'reh piece set to true measure).
rites.effect: Align to natural harmonics. Gain +1 die to actions involving balance, proportion, cadence, or keeping true time.
rites.push_it: The resonance sharpens to a faultless edge: gain +2 dice instead, but mark 1 SB (Diamonds) as nearby imperfect forms momentarily warp. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Perfect Structure
rites.materials: A consecrated compass and straightedge, inked to tolerance.
rites.effect: Impose right form on a chaotic or damaged structure (scaffold, plan, treaty, formation). Treat one structural obstacle as one step easier; gain +1 die to build/repair/spatial-reason rolls.
rites.push_it: Perfection hardens: for the scene the structure gains unnatural resilience, but order draws opposition; mark 1 SB (Clubs). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Resonant Field
rites.materials: A floor or air traced with precise tessellations and nodes (or a Kon'reh board laid out as a focus).
rites.effect: Establish a harmonic field. Allies gain +1 die to precise, coordinated, or team actions; disruptive acts suffer –1 die.
rites.push_it: The field self-sustains and begins to spread beyond intent; mark +1 Obligation. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Pattern's Heart
rites.materials: A solved proof, a perfectly symmetrical token, or a completed Kon'reh puzzle.
rites.effect: Perceive the governing pattern of a complex situation (supply chain, intrigue, weather, battle). Gain +2 dice to prediction/strategy/systemic-understanding and ask two questions about how the pattern drives the present.
rites.push_it: Clarity cuts: gain the insight, but for this scene suffer –1 die to intuitive or purely emotional responses; mark 1 SB (Diamonds). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +2) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Immutable Design
rites.materials: A space gridded for measure and set with true ratios (like a Kon'reh court).
rites.effect: For the scene, rewrite local behavior by perfect principle. Choose one: gravity follows geometric progression; time flows by harmonic ratios; space conforms to exact symmetry.
rites.push_it: Make the design persist, but elsewhere thins and wobbles: mark +2 Obligation and begin Harmonic Dissonance [4]. Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

rites.name: Rite of the Architect's Vision
rites.materials: A ledger of forms and their tuned relations (or a record of Kon'reh victories).
rites.effect: For one session, treat all rolls of pattern recognition, structural analysis, or harmonic application as one step better (Position or Effect, GM's choice).
rites.push_it: Sight devours softness: gain the power, but lose ease with imperfection's beauty; mark Harm 1 (Stress). Cost: Mark +1 Obligation (total +3) or Mark +1 Fatigue.

cantors.name: The Cantor's Song
cantors.description: A Cantor of the Sacred Geometry sings in canons of proportion — melodies that follow strict mathematical progressions, harmonies that lock into resonant frequencies. Their voice is used to measure a room's acoustic perfection, to chart a course through a labyrinth by singing the ratios of its walls, or to lull a chaos-spirit into stillness by singing the geometry of order. The Geometry's Cantors are often found in surveyor's tents, courtrooms, and the halls of mathematicians, humming the laws of form.

cult.name: The Cult of the Unbroken Compass
cult.description: The Geometry's cults gather in places where measure is sacred: ateliers, observatories, courtrooms of appeal, and the quiet rooms where Kon'reh is played. The Unbroken Compass is a fellowship of architects, cartographers, logicians, and Kon'reh masters who believe that the universe is a puzzle that can be solved, and that solving it is the highest form of worship. A ritual of the Cult involves each member laying out a perfect geometric figure (a circle, a square, a golden spiral) and then finding the flaw in another's figure. The one with the fewest flaws leads the next session. Their creed is inscribed on a brass protractor that is never used for mundane measurement: 'Form follows number. Number follows truth. Truth follows no one.' Outsiders are welcome to bring a proof or a problem. If they cannot, they may learn by watching. Among the Ykrul, the same cult takes a different flavor: Kon'reh boards are cut from hide, and the 'pieces' are oath-tokens. A Ykrul geomancer reads the angles of a feud as if they were lines of play, and a victory in Kon'reh can settle a blood-debt without steel. Among the Aelinnel, the cult is quieter, more courteous: they use Kon'reh to map the thresholds between fae and mortal, and a solved puzzle can open a hedge-gate that would otherwise remain sealed.

witchcraft.description: A witch who walks with the Sacred Geometry is a compass-scribe or grid-weaver — one who crafts the tools of perfect form: a square that never warps, a level that finds true horizontal even on a ship at sea, or a set of Kon'reh pieces carved from the same block of stone so that they resonate together. Their magic works through the materials of measurement: a protractor that reveals hidden angles, a string that stretches to an exact ratio, or a lens that shows the underlying grid of a forest floor. They do not use hammers; they use scribes, rules, and the patient turning of a brass dial.

protractor.name: The Protractor of Unseen Measures
protractor.description: A brass semicircle etched with glyphs of ratio and proportion, its hinge oiled with a drop of mercury. Once per session, the witch may place the protractor against any surface (a doorframe, a contract, a creature's brow) and read the 'true angle' of its nature. For the next scene, the witch gains +2 dice to any roll that exploits a hidden weakness, structural flaw, or imbalance revealed by the measurement. The protractor can also be used to 'true' a distorted measurement: touching it to a warped scale or a crooked ruler resets it to perfect accuracy for one use.

hedge_gifts.name: Compass of True Bearing
hedge_gifts.description: Once per scene, the witch may draw a circle with a compass (a real one, or just their finger) around a small area (a room, a crossroads). Those within the circle gain a perfect sense of direction and distance for the next hour; they are immune to mundane spatial confusion (mazes, fog, unfamiliar streets). However, the compass point leaves a faint, glowing mark on the ground that can be seen by anyone with supernatural senses.

corruption.benefit: Harmonic Sense: +1 die to Notice when patterns, ratios, or symmetries matter.
corruption.cost: Pattern Obsession: Must measure or set-right pleasing patterns you spot; resisting costs time or 1 SB (Diamonds).

corruption.benefit: Perfect Precision: Once/scene, achieve faultless calculation or measure.
corruption.cost: Social Dissonance: –1 die to emotive social rolls; harmony feels truer than feeling.

corruption.benefit: Structural Insight: +2 dice to architecture/engineering or imposing order on chaos.
corruption.cost: Order Compulsion: Must correct deliberate disorder; suffer Fatigue 1 when forced to abide it.

corruption.benefit: Universal Harmony: Once/session, apply a harmonic rule that makes a chaotic situation predictable.
corruption.cost: Perfectionist Limitation: –1 die to improvisation; the 'correct' solution calls.

corruption.benefit: Divine Proportion: Once/session, see perfect ratios in any phenomenon and work with them.
corruption.cost: Mathematical Focus: –1 die to acts not readily quantified; the abstract blurs unless counted.

corruption.benefit: Architect of Reality: Once/session, briefly refit local reality by perfect principle.
corruption.cost: Form Prison: Mark +3 Obligation; risk failing in rough, noisy worlds, taking Harm 1 in chaotic zones. You cannot willingly enter a space that is not geometrically 'pure' (e.g., a cave, a tent, a ruin) without first performing a measuring rite (1 minute, no roll).

playstyle_notes.description: The Sacred Geometry suits makers and marshals who tune chaos into form. Its rites prize clean plans, crisp teamwork, and seeing the load paths hidden in trouble. The cost is rigidity: the more you perfect, the harder rough edges are to bear. For Ykrul players, the Geometry is a tool of honor and battle-field geometry; for Aelinnel players, a tool of courtesy and threshold-walking. Both are valid, and both lead to the same cold, beautiful truth.
emphasizes: Harmonic Beauty: power born of right relation
Pattern Mastery: reading the frame beneath events
Structural Perfection: setting things true under strain
Resonant Practice: working with the world's natural measures
Architect's Sight: finding where form should be imposed
Kon'reh Discipline: the game as living geometry, teaching patience, sacrifice, and the dance of Apex and Sanctum

monastic_tradition.name: Measure-Fist (Sacred Geometry)
monastic_tradition.quote: Form follows number. Number follows truth.
monastic_tradition.prerequisites: Wits 3+, Spirit 2+, Rite of Harmonic Alignment or Cantor of Sacred Geometry.
monastic_tradition.debt_resistant_frame: Perfect ratios cannot be bound by imperfect contracts. Monks of the Measure-Fist gain +1 die to resist any magical debt or obligation that relies on asymmetry or flawed logic — they find the imbalance and step through it. The creditor's arithmetic briefly stutters.

techniques.name: Golden Ratio Strike
techniques.effect: Your first unarmed strike each scene gains +1 Effect. Your second gains +1 die. Your third triggers Perfect Alignment (+2 dice, ignore 1 Armor). Sequence resets on a miss.

techniques.name: Harmonic Alignment
techniques.effect: +1 die to actions involving balance, proportion, cadence, or keeping true time.

techniques.name: Perfect Structure
techniques.effect: Impose right form on a chaotic or damaged structure. Treat one structural obstacle as one step easier; gain +1 die to build/repair/spatial-reason rolls.

techniques.name: Pattern's Heart
techniques.effect: Perceive the governing pattern of a complex situation. Gain +2 dice to prediction/strategy and ask two questions about how the pattern drives the present.

techniques.name: Resonant Field
techniques.effect: Establish a harmonic field in a zone. Allies gain +1 die to precise, coordinated, or team actions; disruptive acts suffer –1 die.

master_technique.name: Architect of Reality
master_technique.description: Once per session, briefly refit local reality by perfect principle. You may ignore one environmental penalty entirely, or reroute a debt obligation as if the numbers had always been different (the creditor's ledger shows a different debtor). After the scene, you gain a permanent Debt Scar (your eyes are mildly faceted, like cut gems). Thereafter, once per arc, you may treat a failure on a skill roll involving measurement, prediction, or analysis as a success — but you must correct the balance by performing a pure, selfless act of symmetry (e.g., returning a stolen item, repairing a broken wall).

sample_adventure.title: The Unfinished Proof
sample_adventure.description: A Kon'reh master has discovered a pattern in the game's geometry that seems to predict the movement of the Hollow. She is convinced that if she can complete the proof, she can map the Hollow's attention and avoid its influence entirely. But the proof is incomplete, and the Hollow is taking notice—her workshop is visited by figures who are not quite there, and her Kon'reh pieces move at night. The party must help her complete the proof, protect her from the Hollow's agents, and decide whether a map of the Hollow's attention is a tool of liberation or a trap that will draw its gaze to all who see it.
sample_adventure.quote: The line that maps the Hollow's path is also the line that invites it to follow. To trace the geometry of what you fear is to risk drawing it closer.
